content:
Post 1

ASX crossed its 200-day MA Friday, closing at 8,806 +0.50%. But the real story: Materials surged 2.32% while Health Care shed 1.96%. A split session where the index headline flatters the underlying tension. #ASX #ausbiz

Post 2

⛏️ Materials +2.32% led all sectors, uranium and copper names driving the charge. Health Care -1.96% was the clear laggard — PME alone dropped 6.34%. Eight of eleven sectors closed negative. Breadth was poor beneath the surface. #ASX #materials #healthcare

Post 3

PME fell AUD 13.35 to AUD 197.07, down 6.34% — the sharpest single-stock drag on the index. Against that, SLX surged 9.40% to AUD 5.70. Two extremes in one session that capture the market's indecision perfectly. #ASX #ausbiz

Post 4

🪙 Gold at AUD 5,929.91/oz with AUD/USD at 0.6944. Silver AUD 87.13/oz, platinum AUD 2,428.56/oz. Precious metals strength at these levels explains Materials outperforming every other sector today. #gold #silver #metals #ASX

Post 5

ASX sits 4.31% below its 52-week high after a weekly loss of 0.43%. Can Materials hold above +2% momentum into next week, or does weak breadth reassert? Where do you see the index heading — retest of highs or fade? #ASX #ausbiz #investing
